description: Search the human insulin gene sequence (~/UGENE_Data/human_insulin_gene.gb) for five specific DNA regulatory motifs on both strands: TATA_box ('TATAAA', Exact/Subsequence), E_box ('CANNTG', IUPAC ambiguity codes), GC_box ('GGGCGG', Exact/Subsequence), polyA_signal ('AATAAA', Exact/Subsequence), and CArG_box ('CC[AT]{6}GG', Regular expression). Annotate all occurrences using the exact motif names as the annotation name/group. Export the annotated sequence in GenBank format to ~/UGENE_Data/results/insulin_motifs_annotated.gb. Produce a summary report at ~/UGENE_Data/results/motif_report.txt containing the sequence name, total length, total count of all motifs found, and a 2-4 sentence biological interpretation of the regulatory elements' significance for insulin gene regulation in pancreatic beta cells. For each of the five motifs, the report must include the pattern used, search mode, hit count, and a list of hit positions (start..end).
